name: Kiffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the butter, sour cream, and sugar until smooth.
2. Add egg yolks to the mixture and stir until combined.
3. Gradually add flour, mixing with hands until well blended. Add a little more flour if needed for a stiff dough.
4. Divide the dough into 6-8 sections. Pat each section into a flat disk and chill in the refrigerator.
5. Roll out each chilled section between waxed paper to form a pie crust-like shape.
6. Cut each rolled-out section into 4 triangular pieces using a sharp knife.
7. Place half a teaspoon of prune butter onto each triangle.
8. Roll up each triangle starting at the wide end, forming a log.
9. Arrange the logs on a greased cookie sheet.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) until lightly browned. Optionally, sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving.
These kiffles have very little sugar but are delicious and can give kolaches some stiff competition.
